channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Milwaukee Bucks
channel.description: Milwaukee Bucks trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo: I appreciate you
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Giannis Antetokounmpo: I appreciate you 🙏🏽💯 x.com 90043049007

item.title: Shams Charania: Giannis Antetokounmpo is expected to tell the Bucks 'no' when asked to commit long-term
item.description: Antetokounmpo has one guaranteed year remaining on his contract, giving him considerable leverage over his next destination. The Miami Heat, Golden State Warriors, Los Angeles Lakers, Minnesota Timberwolves and Cleveland Cavaliers are among the teams expected to pursue him. "Any conversation that the Bucks are going to have with Giannis Antetokounmpo, from my understanding, is going to be a very simple one: 'Do you want to stay here long-term and commit long-term in June?'" Charania said on the

item.title: Front office maintaining asking price of "a young …
item.description: Underdog NBA: Latest on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade front, per @ShamsCharania : - Front office maintaining asking price of "a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks" - Bucks also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Giannis to stay once again - Wolves, Cavs, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers pursued Giannis at the trade deadline

item.title: "Sources tell me that the Milwaukee Bucks are fully …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "Sources tell me that the Milwaukee Bucks are fully open for business on trade calls and offers for Giannis Antetokounmpo.. He's got just one guaranteed year left on his deal and he has a ton of leverage on where he ends up"

item.title: Bucks open to trade offers for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Shams Charania: The Milwaukee Bucks are open for business on trade calls and offers for two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo entering the NBA combine and over six weeks away from the draft, sources told ESPN.

item.title: There is expected to be a robust market for …
item.description: The Milwaukee Bucks are open for business on trade calls and offers for two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo entering the NBA draft combine and over six weeks away from the draft, league and team sources told ESPN. There is expected to be a robust market for Antetokounmpo, and ownership and front-office officials expect to maintain their trade deadline asking price of a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks, sources said.

item.title: Nothing has changed in Antetokounmpo's stance that the …
item.description: The Bucks engaged in Antetokounmpo trade talks before the February deadline as the franchise cornerstone believed for a period of months that the time had come to part ways. They now will again after co-owner Jimmy Haslam stated a resolution is coming before the draft on June 23 and 24. Nothing has changed in Antetokounmpo's stance that the time has come for both sides to move on, sources said.

item.title: Bucks leaving door open to acquire superstar in trade to convince Giannis Antetokounmpo to stay
item.description: The Bucks are also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Antetokounmpo to stay once again, allowing Horst to work parallel paths. They will have up to three first-round picks and tradeable contracts such as Bobby Portis and Kyle Kuzma to offer in deals this offseason. One of those first-rounders is the less favorable of their own or the New Orleans Pelicans' this year.

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: "The conversation will be simple: Where does [Giannis] …
item.description: Rival executives believe that Antetokounmpo's desired teams will be the largest factor in his trade destination. He is essentially on an expiring contract with next season guaranteed in his deal before a player option in 2027, providing him leverage to navigate to a specific team based on whether he would agree to stay long term given the players and picks needed to acquire him. He becomes eligible for a four-year, $275 million contract extension Oct. 1 if he is not traded, or six months after b

item.title: Over messages, he told her how fun it was to own a …
item.description: Wesley Edens, the co-founder of Fortress Investment Group and co-owner of the Milwaukee Bucks, answered a LinkedIn message from a China-born entrepreneur in 2022 that blossomed into a correspondence. Changli “Sophia” Luo was a divorcée living in New York City who had founded One World Initiative Advocacy, a Manhattan-based nonprofit that said it worked to produce video interviews with economists and environmentalists. Edens, a billionaire who had divorced his longtime wife the previous year, bu

item.title: Edens, 64, hasn’t been named publicly as the target of …
item.description: Luo, 46, was indicted last year for allegedly trying to shake down Edens for more than $1 billion by threatening to publicize videos and photos of him having sex with her. Her litany of threats continued for months, as Luo reached out to Edens’s family members, told him she would approach investors, and pledged to destroy him, prosecutors said. She was charged with four counts, including blackmail and destruction of records, and has pleaded not guilty. Luo, who was released on a $500,000 bond an

item.title: She sent him a love letter. “I never told you I love …
item.description: After Luo and Edens had sex, prosecutors said, she sent him a love letter. “I never told you I love you, and tonight I want to tell you that, I have been restraining my feeling for you, as I do love you from the bottom of my heart!” Luo wrote. Edens didn’t respond. Months later, Luo’s communications turned from flattering to threatening, according to prosecutors. In November 2023, they said, Luo used a fake name to gain access to the doctor’s office where Edens’s then-girlfriend worked. Luo told

item.title: While Edens denied Luo’s allegations, he agreed to …
item.description: Months later, they said, she wrote to Edens alleging that he had sex with her while she was mentally incapacitated. She told him that her “home has cameras,” and that everything Edens “did was caught on camera.” She would go to the media, she said, unless he apologized. “I am sure your family and business partners will learn about you and your misdeeds from these interviews and will provide exposure that will taint your record forever,” she wrote. She gave him information for a mediator. While E

item.title: Only 10 teams that could make a real run at Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Multiple league sources told the Journal Sentinel that while 29 other teams would of course be “interested” in Antetokounmpo, there may be about 10 teams that could truly make a run at the two-time MVP. Heading into the February trade deadline, team sources maintained to the Journal Sentinel that any potential deal for the star would require “everything” in terms of young players and future draft assets. If the Bucks maintain such a firm stance on a big return might be more indicative of the tea

item.title: "Listen, Jimmy, Wes, myself, Taylor [Jenkins], we're …
item.description: Offers to the Bucks, which began piling up during the season in advance of the February trade deadline, will be resubmitted or refurbished and co-owners Wes Edens and Jimmy and Dee Haslam will have to make a call on the immediate direction of the franchise. "Listen, Jimmy, Wes, myself, Taylor [Jenkins], we're all aligned on how we want to approach this," Bucks GM Jon Horst said at the NBA draft lottery on May 10. "We have an unbelievable opportunity with this [No. 10] pick, with our roster, with

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks GM Jon Horst says trading or keeping Giannis Antetokounmpo is still on the table
item.description: Jon Horst: Just got out of the NBA Draft lottery drawing room, and talking to #Bucks GM Jon Horst. The "two paths" of trading/keeping Giannis Antetokounmpo are obviously both still on the table. Horst was happy the team didn't fall in the draft, which was more probable than moving up.

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: If the Warriors do somehow get their hands on another …
item.description: Another reason to trust Kerr: If the Warriors do somehow get their hands on another star — and you can bet a Lacob-run franchise will try — then Golden State will require Kerr’s greatest strength of maximizing excellence. In the alternate universe where the Warriors land Giannis Antetokounmpo or Kawhi Leonard or LeBron James, Kerr is the coach you’d want. Go watch “Court of Gold” on Netflix and see why.

item.title: Shams Charania: Giannis Antetokounmpo is expected to tell the Bucks 'no' when asked to commit long-term
item.description: Antetokounmpo has one guaranteed year remaining on his contract, giving him considerable leverage over his next destination. The Miami Heat, Golden State Warriors, Los Angeles Lakers, Minnesota Timberwolves and Cleveland Cavaliers are among the teams expected to pursue him. "Any conversation that the Bucks are going to have with Giannis Antetokounmpo, from my understanding, is going to be a very simple one: 'Do you want to stay here long-term and commit long-term in June?'" Charania said on the

item.description: Underdog NBA: Latest on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade front, per @ShamsCharania : - Front office maintaining asking price of "a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks" - Bucks also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Giannis to stay once again - Wolves, Cavs, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers pursued Giannis at the trade deadline

item.title: "Sources tell me that the Milwaukee Bucks are fully …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "Sources tell me that the Milwaukee Bucks are fully open for business on trade calls and offers for Giannis Antetokounmpo.. He's got just one guaranteed year left on his deal and he has a ton of leverage on where he ends up"

item.title: Bucks open to trade offers for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Shams Charania: The Milwaukee Bucks are open for business on trade calls and offers for two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo entering the NBA combine and over six weeks away from the draft, sources told ESPN.

item.title: There is expected to be a robust market for …
item.description: The Milwaukee Bucks are open for business on trade calls and offers for two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo entering the NBA draft combine and over six weeks away from the draft, league and team sources told ESPN. There is expected to be a robust market for Antetokounmpo, and ownership and front-office officials expect to maintain their trade deadline asking price of a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks, sources said.

item.title: Nothing has changed in Antetokounmpo's stance that the …
item.description: The Bucks engaged in Antetokounmpo trade talks before the February deadline as the franchise cornerstone believed for a period of months that the time had come to part ways. They now will again after co-owner Jimmy Haslam stated a resolution is coming before the draft on June 23 and 24. Nothing has changed in Antetokounmpo's stance that the time has come for both sides to move on, sources said.

item.title: Bucks leaving door open to acquire superstar in trade to convince Giannis Antetokounmpo to stay
item.description: The Bucks are also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Antetokounmpo to stay once again, allowing Horst to work parallel paths. They will have up to three first-round picks and tradeable contracts such as Bobby Portis and Kyle Kuzma to offer in deals this offseason. One of those first-rounders is the less favorable of their own or the New Orleans Pelicans' this year.

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: "The conversation will be simple: Where does [Giannis] …
item.description: Rival executives believe that Antetokounmpo's desired teams will be the largest factor in his trade destination. He is essentially on an expiring contract with next season guaranteed in his deal before a player option in 2027, providing him leverage to navigate to a specific team based on whether he would agree to stay long term given the players and picks needed to acquire him. He becomes eligible for a four-year, $275 million contract extension Oct. 1 if he is not traded, or six months after b

item.title: Over messages, he told her how fun it was to own a …
item.description: Wesley Edens, the co-founder of Fortress Investment Group and co-owner of the Milwaukee Bucks, answered a LinkedIn message from a China-born entrepreneur in 2022 that blossomed into a correspondence. Changli “Sophia” Luo was a divorcée living in New York City who had founded One World Initiative Advocacy, a Manhattan-based nonprofit that said it worked to produce video interviews with economists and environmentalists. Edens, a billionaire who had divorced his longtime wife the previous year, bu

item.title: Edens, 64, hasn’t been named publicly as the target of …
item.description: Luo, 46, was indicted last year for allegedly trying to shake down Edens for more than $1 billion by threatening to publicize videos and photos of him having sex with her. Her litany of threats continued for months, as Luo reached out to Edens’s family members, told him she would approach investors, and pledged to destroy him, prosecutors said. She was charged with four counts, including blackmail and destruction of records, and has pleaded not guilty. Luo, who was released on a $500,000 bond an

item.title: She sent him a love letter. “I never told you I love …
item.description: After Luo and Edens had sex, prosecutors said, she sent him a love letter. “I never told you I love you, and tonight I want to tell you that, I have been restraining my feeling for you, as I do love you from the bottom of my heart!” Luo wrote. Edens didn’t respond. Months later, Luo’s communications turned from flattering to threatening, according to prosecutors. In November 2023, they said, Luo used a fake name to gain access to the doctor’s office where Edens’s then-girlfriend worked. Luo told

item.title: While Edens denied Luo’s allegations, he agreed to …
item.description: Months later, they said, she wrote to Edens alleging that he had sex with her while she was mentally incapacitated. She told him that her “home has cameras,” and that everything Edens “did was caught on camera.” She would go to the media, she said, unless he apologized. “I am sure your family and business partners will learn about you and your misdeeds from these interviews and will provide exposure that will taint your record forever,” she wrote. She gave him information for a mediator. While E

item.title: Only 10 teams that could make a real run at Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Multiple league sources told the Journal Sentinel that while 29 other teams would of course be “interested” in Antetokounmpo, there may be about 10 teams that could truly make a run at the two-time MVP. Heading into the February trade deadline, team sources maintained to the Journal Sentinel that any potential deal for the star would require “everything” in terms of young players and future draft assets. If the Bucks maintain such a firm stance on a big return might be more indicative of the tea

item.title: "Listen, Jimmy, Wes, myself, Taylor [Jenkins], we're …
item.description: Offers to the Bucks, which began piling up during the season in advance of the February trade deadline, will be resubmitted or refurbished and co-owners Wes Edens and Jimmy and Dee Haslam will have to make a call on the immediate direction of the franchise. "Listen, Jimmy, Wes, myself, Taylor [Jenkins], we're all aligned on how we want to approach this," Bucks GM Jon Horst said at the NBA draft lottery on May 10. "We have an unbelievable opportunity with this [No. 10] pick, with our roster, with

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks GM Jon Horst says trading or keeping Giannis Antetokounmpo is still on the table
item.description: Jon Horst: Just got out of the NBA Draft lottery drawing room, and talking to #Bucks GM Jon Horst. The "two paths" of trading/keeping Giannis Antetokounmpo are obviously both still on the table. Horst was happy the team didn't fall in the draft, which was more probable than moving up.

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: If the Warriors do somehow get their hands on another …
item.description: Another reason to trust Kerr: If the Warriors do somehow get their hands on another star — and you can bet a Lacob-run franchise will try — then Golden State will require Kerr’s greatest strength of maximizing excellence. In the alternate universe where the Warriors land Giannis Antetokounmpo or Kawhi Leonard or LeBron James, Kerr is the coach you’d want. Go watch “Court of Gold” on Netflix and see why.

item.title: The NBA concluded its investigation into the Milwaukee …
item.description: The NBA concluded its investigation into the Milwaukee Bucks over the shutdown of Giannis Antetokounmpo and has determined that no further action is warranted, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania on Tuesday. The league launched its probe early last month after Antetokounmpo and the Bucks told different stories about his ability to return to the court. Antetokounmpo hyperextended his left knee on March 15 and did not appear again for the Bucks during the regular season.

item.title: The Bucks told investigators that Antetokounmpo …
item.description: Antetokounmpo said multiple times that he was healthy enough to return before the end of the season but that the team would not medically clear him. Milwaukee, meanwhile, informed the league that it didn't believe Antetokounmpo wanted to actually play. The Bucks told investigators that Antetokounmpo declined the opportunity to play 3-on-3 scrimmages as part of the return protocol from his knee injury. Antetokounmpo told the league that he was healthy enough to return to action regardless.

item.title: NBA takes no action after investigating Giannis Antetokounmpo’s Bucks shutdown
item.description: Shams Charania: The NBA concluded its investigation into the Milwaukee Bucks over the shutdown and health of Giannis Antetokounmpo late in the season and has determined that no further action is warranted, sources tell ESPN
